title: verbascum armenum
intro: Verbascum armenum, also known as Armenian mullein, is a biennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Armenia, Turkey, and the Caucasus. Its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Armenian mullein include soothing respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as alleviating skin irritations and wounds. Armenian mullein can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
